jokes.text: Then he died.
jokes.setup: A wise man said nothing really dies, setting up philosophical wisdom
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercutting the wisdom by noting the wise man died
jokes.explanation: Economical ironic reversal that immediately deflates the opening's pretension. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: ♪ Jumpin' Joe and golly jeeper / I got one gigantic peeper / Sassafras and banana oil / I'm a beautiful purple-haired goil. ♪
jokes.setup: 1930s-style jaunty song with nonsensical lyrics
jokes.punchline: Leela describing her one eye as a 'gigantic peeper' in period slang
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that plays on Leela's cyclops nature within the period pastiche setting.

jokes.text: Do you think a space-age dame like her would ever marry a two-bit low-life delivery boy like me, and also I have bad posture and severe financial problems?
jokes.setup: Fry asking for romantic advice about Leela
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly pathetic details about himself after the main question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure that builds on Fry's self-deprecation, though the concept is familiar.

jokes.text: Have you tried getting her pregnant? / Gosh, yes, I've tried and tried, but so far I only got Amy pregnant.
jokes.setup: Bender's crude romantic advice
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact admission about accidentally getting the wrong person pregnant
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist misdirection with perfect deadpan delivery. The casual admission of getting Amy pregnant is genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: That's for sure, not on the measly salary I steal from you each month.
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting he can't afford a ring on his salary
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting he steals his salary rather than earns it
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony that reveals Fry's casual dishonesty while maintaining his sympathetic nature.

jokes.text: Mmm-mmm, fish on Friday and human flesh the rest of the week.
jokes.setup: Casual comment about weekly diet
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-factly mentioning cannibalism as routine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - casual cannibalism delivered with his characteristic cheerful obliviousness.

jokes.text: Hang on, let me just adjust it to your eye level.
jokes.setup: Professor adjusting telescope for Leela to look through
jokes.punchline: Visual implication that he's adjusting it for her single eye in the center of her head
jokes.explanation: Subtle visual gag that plays on Leela's unique anatomy without being heavy-handed.

jokes.text: Oh, fuff. Nothing in the universe can fracture diamondium, not even God One and God Two put together.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining the indestructible nature of diamondium
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing multiple gods as a unit of measurement for cosmic power
jokes.explanation: Creative worldbuilding joke that treats multiple deities as a casual reference point for strength.

jokes.text: Why, it's lit up like a smooth, refreshing Chesterfield.
jokes.setup: Describing the brightness of a comet
jokes.punchline: Using a cigarette brand as comparison in 1930s advertising speak
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate advertising language that fits the segment's style, though not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: You're boopin' my betty.
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about being disturbed
jokes.punchline: Using period slang to describe interference in a way that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Good use of period slang with mild innuendo, economical delivery.

jokes.text: I got to go check this comet for anarchists.
jokes.setup: Bender making an excuse to leave
jokes.punchline: Claiming to check a space comet for 1930s political threats
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination of space-age setting with period-appropriate paranoia about anarchists.

jokes.text: All right, diamond, you won those rounds, but I've got an ace up my hole.
jokes.setup: Fry addressing the diamond he's trying to break
jokes.punchline: Mangling the phrase 'ace up my sleeve' in a crude way
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry malapropism that works for his character but relies on crude wordplay.

jokes.text: Good? Huh, I-I guess they can.
jokes.setup: Fry says words can't describe how he feels, Leela suggests 'Good?'
jokes.punchline: Fry realizes words actually can describe his feelings perfectly well
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of romantic cliché with Fry's surprised self-awareness

jokes.text: I thought you snuck off to take a dump. / A man can sneak off to do two things.
jokes.setup: Leela's crude assumption about where Fry went
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that defecation is one of two sneaking-off activities
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on romantic vs. crude expectations with Fry's typical obtuseness.

jokes.text: You've made me the happiest finger in the whole wide hand.
jokes.setup: Leela expressing joy about the engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Speaking from the perspective of her finger rather than herself
jokes.explanation: Creative perspective shift that's both romantic and absurd, good economy of language.

jokes.text: You've made me the happiest finger in the whole wide hand.
jokes.setup: Traditional romantic acceptance response
jokes.punchline: Body part metaphor taken literally and extended awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that extends the finger/ring metaphor but doesn't land strongly

jokes.text: Comet kaboomination in three, two... Um, hello, cuckoo.
jokes.setup: Dramatic countdown for explosion
jokes.punchline: Interruption by what's presumably a cuckoo clock, deflating the tension
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax structure with period-appropriate wordplay, though the interruption feels somewhat arbitrary.

jokes.text: It went kersplitters.
jokes.setup: The diamond breaking into pieces
jokes.punchline: Fry's made-up onomatopoeia combining 'kerplunk' and 'splinters'
jokes.explanation: Creative Fry-ism that feels authentic to his character's tendency to mangle language.

jokes.text: Fry, you've created a new color, totally different from any other color or combination of colors.
jokes.setup: The broken diamond creating a rainbow
jokes.punchline: Claiming a genuinely new color exists, which is scientifically impossible
jokes.explanation: Clever impossible concept that plays with our understanding of color theory in a sci-fi context.

jokes.text: Gornella, my love, will you marry me? / Blorg, blorg, a thousand times blorg.
jokes.setup: Alien version of Fry's proposal
jokes.punchline: Alien Leela's response using 'blorg' instead of 'yes' in the classic romantic formula
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure that maintains the romantic beat while adding alien absurdity.

jokes.text: Hey, Professor. Yes, Bender. Aah! Boo!
jokes.setup: Professor responding to Bender's call
jokes.punchline: Bender scares Professor for no reason
jokes.explanation: Basic prank humor that fits Bender's character but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Byte my eight-bit metal ass. That's byte with a 'Y.'
jokes.setup: 8-bit video game aesthetic Bender insulting someone
jokes.punchline: Clarifying the computer pun spelling while maintaining his crude catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Excellent adaptation of Bender's signature line to the 8-bit context with clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Whatever you say, mon.
jokes.setup: 8-bit video game version of Hermes
jokes.punchline: His characteristic Jamaican accent reduced to pixelated form
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character consistency joke that works within the 8-bit aesthetic.

jokes.text: Mon, that's some cheap-ass matter.
jokes.setup: Professor about to examine matter under the microscope
jokes.punchline: Hermes immediately judging the quality of matter in his characteristic style
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that applies Hermes's bureaucratic criticism to abstract scientific concepts.

jokes.text: Oh, my, there's a frog on a bump on this log that I found in a hole in the bottom of the sea.
jokes.setup: Professor examining matter under microscope
jokes.punchline: Beginning the classic children's song 'There Was an Old Lady Who Swallowed a Fly' structure
jokes.explanation: Clever integration of familiar song structure into scientific discovery, building anticipation for the pattern.

jokes.text: Dear Liza.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the children's song pattern
jokes.punchline: Amy completing the reference to 'There's a Hole in My Bucket'
jokes.explanation: Perfect two-word callback that rewards viewers who recognize the children's song reference.

jokes.text: Pfft, those things don't rhyme. / Things only rhyme below ten to the minus five angstroms, you dope.
jokes.setup: Amy dismissing scientific terms for not rhyming
jokes.punchline: Professor providing an absurdly specific scientific explanation for when things rhyme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that creates a fake scientific law about rhyming at subatomic scales.

jokes.text: It's a mirror into Scruffy's soul.
jokes.setup: Others marveling at the fundamental structure of the universe
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's characteristically understated personal reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment that contrasts cosmic significance with his mundane self-reflection.

jokes.text: I like physics, but I love cartoons.
jokes.setup: Reference to Stephen Hawking quitting physics
jokes.punchline: Hawking himself explaining his preference for cartoon voice acting
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Hawking's real-life cartoon appearances, delivered with perfect economy.

jokes.text: I watch TV. It's the next best thing to being alive.
jokes.setup: Professor asking how people cope with meaninglessness
jokes.punchline: Fry's bleak but honest assessment of television
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny existential observation that perfectly captures modern malaise

jokes.text: I checked the invariance of your Lagrangian. / Hubba hubba.
jokes.setup: Amy using complex physics terminology
jokes.punchline: Bender treating the scientific language as sexually suggestive
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor that plays on Bender's tendency to sexualize everything.

jokes.text: Probably magnets. / Shut up, Hawking!
jokes.setup: Fry giving his typical pseudo-scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Professor's exasperated dismissal of the celebrity scientist
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Fry's magnets obsession and Professor's academic jealousy.

jokes.text: Fry, you idiot! You're a genius!
jokes.setup: Professor dismissing Fry then immediately reversing
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of calling someone an idiot and genius simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction structure that captures the Professor's mercurial personality.

jokes.text: It would take decades of work by thousands of scientists in a particle accelerator powered by dump trucks of flaming grant money!
jokes.setup: Describing the enormous task ahead
jokes.punchline: Absurd but accurate description of research funding needs
jokes.explanation: Vivid metaphor for academic funding reality with great imagery

jokes.text: I'm surprised you lived through that sentence.
jokes.setup: Professor delivering an extremely long, complex sentence
jokes.punchline: Fry commenting on the physical impossibility of such lengthy exposition
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor that acknowledges the character's tendency toward verbose exposition.

jokes.text: What is that box, you old witch? Ha, ha, ha. I'll never tell you. It's a deep space emotion detector.
jokes.setup: Professor claiming he'll never reveal the box's purpose
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells them exactly what it is
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor behavior - dramatic secrecy immediately undermined

jokes.text: Flying bananas? / Ha, ha. I will slice them on my morning fish porridge.
jokes.setup: Alien threat appearing as flying bananas
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's casual culinary response to the danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment that treats cosmic threat as breakfast ingredient.

jokes.text: My shell may be tough, like a samurai honeymoon mask, but inside I'm as soft and sensitive as a girl made of custard.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg defending his diplomatic qualifications
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd metaphors comparing himself to impossible objects
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist imagery with 'samurai honeymoon mask' and 'girl made of custard' being genuinely creative and memorable.

jokes.text: Custard time? / Hooray!
jokes.setup: Fry mishearing Zoidberg's custard metaphor
jokes.punchline: Everyone getting excited about literal custard time
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that shows the crew's food obsession overriding serious moments.

jokes.text: I see your mother the water mutant didn't raise any fools, Turanga Leela.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Leela's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment referencing her mutant heritage
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Leela's background while delivering a compliment

jokes.text: Me, me. Choose Zoidberg. Zoidberg a diplomat? The list of things I've heard now contains everything.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg volunteering for diplomatic mission
jokes.punchline: Leela's complete disbelief expressed as hyperbolic inventory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hyperbolic expression of disbelief that's highly quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: Custard time? Hooray!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg mentioning custard in serious context
jokes.punchline: Fry's childlike excitement about dessert possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - missing the point to focus on food

jokes.text: What if we hire a buttered geisha? Another one of your ill-timed jokes, Fry? You and I are enemies now.
jokes.setup: Fry's absurd suggestion for smooth dancing
jokes.punchline: Leela's extreme overreaction to declare enmity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of anime-style dramatic relationships over minor issues

jokes.text: You may be gelatinous, but not even the Mochi Phantom could perform such intricate motions! / Says you, salaryman.
jokes.setup: Amy doubting Zoidberg's dance abilities with anime-style reference
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg dismissively calling her a salaryman in response
jokes.explanation: Good anime parody with specific cultural references that fit the characters.

jokes.text: I underestimated you, Medical Crab. But, will the Aliens understand?
jokes.setup: Leela acknowledging Zoidberg's success
jokes.punchline: Still referring to him by species rather than name
jokes.explanation: Maintains the running gag of treating Zoidberg as species representative
